page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by Intel processors with 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance utilizes dedicated CPU allocation and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource contention.

The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not feature local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. There are no onboard GPUs. Network connectivity is a key feature of this profile, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and high network throughput, the m8ib.24xlarge is designed for resource-dense general-purpose applications. It is well-suited for enterprise application servers, database hosting, web serving, and containerized microservices that require stable CPU performance and high-speed network communication.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz with dedicated CPU allocation under the Nitro hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This hardware profile makes it suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, database hosting, and general-purpose workloads requiring high network throughput and balanced compute-to-memory ratios.
